message.content:
Here are some dependable tool brands that are great for basic home repairs and small DIY projects. These brands balance price, quality, and availability:

• DeWalt  
Reliable, durable, and widely used. Great if you want long-lasting tools without going overly premium.

• Milwaukee  
High‑quality and powerful. Often more expensive but excellent for anyone who wants tools that will grow with their skills.

• Makita  
Balanced performance and ergonomics. Good for both beginners and experienced DIYers.

• Bosch  
Solid tools, especially for drills, drivers, and measuring devices. Good reputation for accuracy and reliability.

• Craftsman  
Affordable and dependable. Excellent for homeowners who need a complete set without overspending.

• Ryobi  
Budget‑friendly and beginner‑friendly. Huge battery ecosystem, making it easy to expand as you take on more projects.

• Kobalt  
Lowe’s house brand, good value for the price. Their cordless tools are surprisingly solid for casual use.

• Stanley  
Great for basic hand tools like hammers, screwdrivers, and utility knives.

If you want the simplest recommendation:  
For power tools on a budget, go with Ryobi.  
For better performance without breaking the bank, choose DeWalt or Makita.  
For hand tools, Stanley or Craftsman are safe picks.

If you tell me what kinds of projects you expect to do, I can narrow it down further.
